data.q: What is the primary purpose of generators in PHP?
data.e: Generators allow efficient iteration over large datasets without loading everything into memory.

data.q: Which keyword is used to create a generator in PHP?
data.e: Using the yield keyword inside a function turns it into a generator.

data.q: How does a generator differ from a normal function?
o: It returns all values at once
It yields values one at a time
data.e: Generators pause execution and yield values one at a time during iteration.

data.q: What is an iterable in PHP?
o: A value that can be looped over
data.e: An iterable is any value that can be traversed using foreach, such as arrays or Traversable objects.

data.q: Which loop is commonly used to iterate over generators?
data.e: Generators are typically consumed using foreach loops.

data.q: What advantage do generators provide for large datasets?
data.e: Generators reduce memory usage by producing values lazily instead of storing all values at once.

data.q: Which interface is commonly associated with iterable objects in PHP?
data.e: Traversable is the base interface for objects that can be iterated using foreach.

data.q: What happens when a generator function reaches the end?
data.e: Once all yield statements are exhausted, iteration naturally stops.

data.q: Can generators maintain internal state between iterations?
data.e: Generators preserve execution state between yielded values.

data.q: Which keyword is primarily used with iterables in PHP?
data.e: foreach is the primary construct used to iterate over iterable values in PHP.
